title: Month-End Close
subhead: The most important week of an accountant's month.

chunks.title: Why We Close the Books
chunks.bodyHtml:
At the end of every month, the accounting team 'closes' the books. This means finalizing all transactions so leadership can look at accurate financial statements.

If you don't accrue an unpaid electric bill, your Profit & Loss will look artificially good, leading to bad business decisions.

steps.title: Day 1: Cutoff & AP
steps.desc: Stop entering new invoices. Ensure all bills from last month are entered.

steps.title: Day 2: Bank Recon
steps.desc: Reconcile all bank and credit card accounts to the penny.

steps.title: Day 3: Accruals
steps.desc: Post journal entries for expenses incurred but not yet billed (e.g., accrued payroll).

steps.title: Day 4: Depreciation
steps.desc: Record the monthly wear-and-tear on fixed assets.

steps.title: Day 5: Financials
steps.desc: Generate the Profit & Loss and Balance Sheet for management review.

questions.q: What is the primary purpose of an accrual journal entry?
options: To hide cash from the IRS
To record an expense that occurred, even if the bill hasn't arrived
questions.explanation: Accruals ensure expenses are matched to the period they occurred in, regardless of cash flow.
